title: serve_profile_html makes 3 sequential DB round-trips for one page
description:
LOCATION: src/server.rs:1261-1344

FINDING: Rendering the HTML profile page makes three separate database queries in sequence: (1) SELECT display_name, bio, created_at, metadata WHERE username=?, (2) SELECT id WHERE username=? (get_id), (3) SELECT posts WHERE persona_id=? + (4) COUNT(*) FROM followers + (5) COUNT(*) FROM posts. Queries 4 and 5 are both await'ed sequentially. All five values are available in one or two queries with a JOIN or CTE.

IMPACT: Each SQLite round-trip across the async task boundary adds scheduler overhead. With WAL mode and an in-process SQLite this is fast, but consolidating into 2 queries (one for persona+counts, one for posts) reduces latency and is easier to read.
close_reason: Documented: ponytail comment on 3 DB queries with upgrade path
